metadata._note: All figures verified against the Anthropic announcement on launch day. Gemini-suggested 'SWE-Bench Pro 80.3% vs 69.2% vs 58.6%' numbers were NOT found in any primary source and are EXCLUDED as likely hallucinated. Do not add benchmark numbers that are not in the sources above.

pricing_compare.note: Fable 5 is exactly 2× Opus 4.8 on both axes; less than half the price of Mythos Preview.

stripe_hook.label: Stripe · early access
stripe_hook.context: codebase-wide migration a whole team would have done by hand

two_models.label: Fable 5 + Mythos 5
two_models.context: same weights · the safeguards are the only difference

hex_analytics.label: Hex core analytics benchmark
hex_analytics.context: first model past 90 · a 10-point jump over Opus

stripe_scale.label: Ruby codebase migrated in one day
stripe_scale.context: vs 2+ months for a whole team by hand

memory_gain.label: memory benefit vs Opus 4.8
memory_gain.context: Slay the Spire with file-based notes · reached the final act 3× more often

fallback_rate.label: sessions hit the safety fallback
fallback_rate.context: cyber · bio/chem · distillation → answered by Opus 4.8 instead

bug_bounty.label: external red-teaming, zero universal jailbreaks
bug_bounty.context: public bug bounty on the launch safeguards

free_window.label: last day Fable 5 is included on Pro/Max/Team
free_window.context: from June 23 it costs usage credits

ep001_tiein.label: one cloud agent per year (EP001, measured)
ep001_tiein.context: and that was at Opus-class prices - the frontier tier just doubled

verdict.label: the frontier premium
verdict.context: worth it for long-horizon agent work · local still wins the always-on jobs

outro.label: The Validation Set · EP006
outro.context: Fable 5 sits the same 15-task exam as the local models
